DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED A SHORT LIVED TORNADO IMPACTED FAITH ENGINEERING LAST EVENING. 2 BUILDINGS SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE AND HAD WINDOWS BROKEN. A TRAILER WAS FLIPPED. MULTIPLE TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. AND A DUMPSTER WAS LIFTED AND BLOWN ACROSS HWY 69. RATING EF1. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 0.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH 150 YARDS.

TORNADO BEGAN NEAR INTERSECTION OF HWY 69 AND COLD SPRINGS RD AND ENDED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SULZER AND FELD RDS. ALONG THE PATH A BARN AND POLE SHED WERE DESTROYED, 4 HOUSES SUSTAINED DAMAGE, 4 POWER POLES SNAPPED, NUMEROUS TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND A CAMPER WAS ROLLED INTO A POND. RATING EF-1. MAX WINDS 105 MPH. PATH LENGTH APPROX 2.1 MILES.MAX WIDTH 300 YARDS.

RECEIVED A CALL FROM DISPATCH LETTING US KNOW THAT THEY WERE DOING EVACUATIONS IN PORTIONS OF FOND DU LAC COUNTY DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. MRMS RAINFALL ESTIMATES WERE NEARLY 5 INCHES FROM CENTRAL TO NORTHEASTERN PORTIONS OF THE COUNTY.

EF0. ESTIMATED 70-80 MPH. TORNADO STARTED AROUND 1ST STREET AND EAST WASHINGTON AVE, OR HIGHWAY 151, ON EAST SIDE OF MADISON. TRACKED ALONG EAST WASHINGTON AVE, HIGHWAY 151, TO WHERE IT INTERSECTS INTERSTATE I90/39. TRACK LENGTH IS 4.2 MILES. TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 50-100 YARDS WIDE. TIME ON GROUND FROM 459 TO 506 PM, OR 7 MIN.

BASED ON RADAR SIGNATURES,LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS AND DAMAGE IMAGES ON SOCIAL MEDIA, IT APPEARS A TORNADO DEVELOPED ON THE EAST SIDE OF MADISON, ON EAST WASHINGTON AVE AND MOVED NORTHEAST TOWARD SUN PRAIRIE. AT THIS TIME IT IS UNKNOWN HOW FAR THIS TORNADO TRAVELED. THERE IS ROOF AND TREE DAMAGE NOTED IN THE PICTURES. THE TIME IS AN ESTIMATE AND SUBJECT TO CHANGE. THE NWS WILL SEND SOMEONE OUT ON SUNDAY TO SURVEY THE DAMAGE.
STORM CHASER REPORTS TWO LARGE UPROOTED TREES ON HOMES ON RIVER ST AND TWO PONTOON BOATS IN THE WATER FLIPPED UPSIDE DOWN. DISPATCH REPORTED 1 TREE DOWN. MANY SMALL BRANCHES DOWN IN THE AREA.
